name: Lake McConaughy
description:
Lake McConaughy is Nebraska's largest reservoir and is affectionately known as Nebraska's ocean, stretching over 20 miles through the sand hills of western Nebraska. The lake offers outstanding walleye and white bass fishing, with trophy wipers regularly exceeding 15 pounds. Channel catfish thrive in the deeper channels, while smallmouth bass patrol the rocky points and submerged structure throughout the reservoir.

The lake features miles of white sand beaches and excellent boating access with multiple public boat ramps operated by the Nebraska Game and Parks Commission. Kingsley Dam, which impounds the North Platte River to form the reservoir, creates a dramatic backdrop on the eastern end. The surrounding Lake McConaughy State Recreation Area provides camping, swimming, and year-round fishing opportunities that draw anglers from across the Great Plains.

name: Lewis and Clark Lake
description:
Lewis and Clark Lake straddles the Nebraska-South Dakota border, formed by Gavins Point Dam on the Missouri River. This 31,000-acre reservoir is one of the most productive walleye fisheries in the region, with consistent catches of fish in the 18- to 24-inch range. White bass run heavy in the spring, and channel catfish provide excellent action throughout the warmer months in the shallower bays and flats.

Boating access is well-developed on both the Nebraska and South Dakota sides, with several paved boat ramps and marinas offering full services. The lake's relatively shallow depth and fertile waters support strong forage populations that fuel impressive fish growth rates. The tailwaters below Gavins Point Dam are equally popular, offering access to paddlefish, sauger, and trophy catfish in the last free-flowing stretch of the Missouri River.

name: Harlan County Reservoir
description:
Harlan County Reservoir is south-central Nebraska's premier multi-species fishery, impounding the Republican River to create over 13,000 acres of productive fishing water. The reservoir is renowned for its trophy wiper fishing, with hybrid striped bass frequently exceeding 10 pounds. Walleye fishing peaks during spring and fall, and white bass provide fast action when schools push into the shallows chasing shad.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers manages several well-maintained boat ramps and recreation areas around the reservoir's 75-mile shoreline. Crappie fishing is particularly strong in the standing timber and brush piles found in the upper arms of the lake. The surrounding Harlan County Lake area offers camping, hiking, and wildlife viewing, making it a popular destination for families combining outdoor recreation with serious fishing.

name: Calamus Reservoir
description:
Calamus Reservoir sits in the scenic Sandhills region of north-central Nebraska, offering over 5,000 acres of clear water fishing in a peaceful, uncrowded setting. The reservoir is one of Nebraska's top destinations for northern pike, with fish regularly exceeding 30 inches. Walleye provide consistent action along the main channel breaks, while largemouth bass hold in the weed beds and submerged vegetation of the shallower bays.

The Calamus State Recreation Area surrounds the lake and provides paved boat ramps, camping facilities, and scenic overlooks of the rolling Sandhills landscape. Yellow perch fishing is excellent during late fall and winter, drawing ice anglers from across the state. The clear water conditions make Calamus a favorite among anglers who prefer sight-fishing and finesse techniques for bass and panfish.

name: Lake Ogallala
description:
Lake Ogallala is a small but highly productive tailwater lake situated directly below Kingsley Dam at the base of Lake McConaughy. Cold water flowing from the bottom of the larger reservoir creates ideal conditions for rainbow and brown trout, making this one of Nebraska's few quality trout fisheries. The lake's compact 320 acres concentrate fish and create outstanding catch rates for anglers of all skill levels.

Despite its small size, Lake Ogallala provides good boat access with a public ramp, though many anglers prefer fishing from shore along the dam face and outlet channel. The cold, well-oxygenated water also supports walleye and largemouth bass in the warmer sections of the lake. This hidden gem is a must-visit companion to any trip to Lake McConaughy, offering a completely different fishing experience just minutes away.

name: Sherman Reservoir
description:
Sherman Reservoir is a 2,845-acre irrigation reservoir in central Nebraska that punches well above its weight as a fishing destination. The lake is stocked with walleye and wiper, both of which grow quickly on abundant gizzard shad forage. White bass provide explosive topwater action during their spring spawning runs, and crappie stack up in the submerged brush and standing timber along the creek channels.

The Sherman State Recreation Area offers a paved boat ramp, camping, and picnic facilities along the reservoir's 25-mile shoreline. Channel catfish thrive in the fertile waters, with fish over five pounds common during summer nights. The reservoir's central Nebraska location makes it an accessible day trip for anglers from Grand Island, Kearney, and the surrounding communities seeking quality multi-species fishing without the crowds of larger destinations.
